秋祭り（10月17日） |神事= }} '''辛国神社'''（からくにじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺にある[[神社]]。[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== ; 主殿 * [[饒速日命]]（ニギハヤヒノミコト） * [[天児屋根命]]（アマノコヤネノミコト） * [[素盞鳴命]]（スサノオノミコト） ; 相殿 * [[品陀別命]]（ホンダワケノミコト） * [[市杵島姫命]]（イチキシメヒメノミコト） ==歴史== 「幸国」という名称は、かつてこの地を収めていた物部氏の氏族である幸国連から由来している。明治5年に[[村社]]に列せられる。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺1-19-14 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄南大阪線]][[藤井寺駅]]下車後、徒歩約5分（南へ約200m） == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/fujiidera-city/m27_02_fujiidera_karakuni.html 辛國神社 - 大阪府神社庁] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ 辛國神社 公式ホームページ] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からくにしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:藤井寺市の建築物]]

{{神社 |名称=辛国神社 |画像= |所在地=[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 35 | 経度秒 = 37 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神=[[饒速日命]]
[[天児屋根命]]
[[素盞鳴命]] |社格=[[式内小社]]
旧[[村社]] |創建=不詳（雄略天皇の時代か） |本殿=三間社[[流造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= 夏祭り（7月17日）
秋祭り（10月17日） |神事= }} '''辛国神社'''（からくにじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺にある[[神社]]。[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== ; 主殿 * [[饒速日命]]（ニギハヤヒノミコト） * [[天児屋根命]]（アマノコヤネノミコト） * [[素盞鳴命]]（スサノオノミコト） ; 相殿 * [[品陀別命]]（ホンダワケノミコト） * [[市杵島姫命]]（イチキシメヒメノミコト） ==歴史== 「幸国」という名称は、かつてこの地を収めていた物部氏の氏族である幸国連から由来している。明治5年に[[村社]]に列せられる。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺1-19-14 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄南大阪線]][[藤井寺駅]]下車後、徒歩約5分（南へ約200m） == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/fujiidera-city/m27_02_fujiidera_karakuni.html 辛國神社 - 大阪府神社庁] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ 辛國神社 公式ホームページ] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からくにしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:藤井寺市の建築物]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]]

{{神社 |名称=辛国神社 |画像= |所在地=[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 35 | 経度秒 = 37 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神=[[饒速日命]]
[[天児屋根命]]
[[素盞鳴命]] |社格=[[式内小社]]
旧[[村社]] |創建=不詳（雄略天皇の時代か） |本殿=三間社[[流造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= 夏祭り（7月17日）
秋祭り（10月17日） |神事= }} '''辛国神社'''（からくにじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== ; 主殿 * [[饒速日命]]（ニギハヤヒノミコト） * [[天児屋根命]]（アマノコヤネノミコト） * [[素盞鳴命]]（スサノオノミコト） ; 相殿 * [[品陀別命]]（ホンダワケノミコト） * [[市杵島姫命]]（イチキシメヒメノミコト） ==歴史== 「幸国」という名称は、かつてこの地を収めていた[[物部氏]]の一支族であり、[[役小角]]の弟子であり呪術者の[[韓国広足]]を輩出した幸国連から由来している。創建は不詳であるが、[[日本書紀]]の[[雄略天皇]]13年3月条に「餌香長野邑を物部大連に賜う」とあることから、その頃に物部氏の祖先である饒速日命を祭ったものが始まりと考えられる「藤井寺市史」第一巻通史編一638頁（1997年）。日本三代実録には[[867年]]（貞観9年）9月26日に官社に列せられたという記述があり、[[延喜式神名帳]]では小社に列している。 中世に入り、河内守護である[[畠山基国]]が社領200石を寄進し、現在の地へ社殿を造営すると同時に奈良の[[春日大社]]から天児屋根命を合祀したことから、近世にかけて「春日社」と称すようになった{{refnest|group=注釈|神社近くの「春日丘」という地名は、そこから由来するものである[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html 辛國神社（からくにじんじゃ） - 藤井寺市観光協会]2018年1月30日　閲覧。}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ 南河内屈指の長さと美しさを持つ参道　辛国神社のご紹介　〜藤井寺市〜]2018年1月30日　閲覧。[[大乗院寺社雑事記]]の[[1458年]]（長禄2年）7月1日条には神主職は岡村入道及びその先祖が代々[[興福寺]]から補任されており、神主が岡村庄の[[検断権]]を持っていたという記述がある。 明治に入ってから現在の「辛国神社」と改称され、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に[[村社]]に列せられる。[[1907年]]（明治40年）に野中村にあった村社野中神社、翌年には[[葛井寺]]内にあった藤井寺字長野の村社長野神社を合祀する。これによって長野神社の祭神であった素盞鳴命も合祀された。その後、野中神社は[[1950年]]（昭和25年）頃に復社されている。 ==境内== 境内から入って深い緑に覆われた長い参道は1989年に「大阪みどりの百選」に選ばれている。また、参道には合祀された「長野神社」と彫られた鳥居があり、これは長野神社より移設されたものである「藤井寺市史」第一巻通史編一635頁（1997年）。 === 境内社 === *春日稲荷神社（祭神：[[宇迦之御魂大神]]） *春日天満宮（祭神：[[菅原道真]]公） == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺1-19-14 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄南大阪線]][[藤井寺駅]]下車後、徒歩約8分（南東へ約600m） == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/fujiidera-city/m27_02_fujiidera_karakuni.html 辛國神社 - 大阪府神社庁] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ 辛國神社 公式ホームページ] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からくにしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:藤井寺市の建築物]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]]

秋祭り（10月17日） |神事= }} '''辛国神社'''（からくにじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== ; 主殿 * [[饒速日命]]（ニギハヤヒノミコト） * [[天児屋根命]]（アマノコヤネノミコト） * [[素盞鳴命]]（スサノオノミコト） ; 相殿 * [[品陀別命]]（ホンダワケノミコト） * [[市杵島姫命]]（イチキシメヒメノミコト） ==歴史== 「幸国」という名称は、かつてこの地を収めていた[[物部氏]]の一支族であり、[[役小角]]の弟子であり呪術者の[[韓国広足]]を輩出した幸国連から由来している。創建は不詳であるが、[[日本書紀]]の[[雄略天皇]]13年3月条に「餌香長野邑を物部大連に賜う」とあることから、その頃に領地を賜った物部氏が祖先である饒速日命を祀ったことから始まり、後に幸国連が祭祀を司ったものと考えられる「藤井寺市史」第一巻通史編一638頁（1997年）。[[日本三代実録]]には[[867年]]（貞観9年）9月26日に官社に列せられたという記述があり、[[延喜式神名帳]]では小社に列している。 中世に入り、河内守護である[[畠山基国]]が社領200石を寄進し、現在の地へ社殿を造営すると同時に奈良の[[春日大社]]から天児屋根命を合祀したことから、近世にかけて「春日社」と称すようになった{{refnest|group=注釈|神社近くの「春日丘」という地名は、そこから由来するものである[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html 辛國神社（からくにじんじゃ） - 藤井寺市観光協会]2018年1月30日　閲覧。}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ 南河内屈指の長さと美しさを持つ参道　辛国神社のご紹介　〜藤井寺市〜]2018年1月30日　閲覧。[[大乗院寺社雑事記]]の[[1458年]]（長禄2年）7月1日条には神主職は岡村入道及びその先祖が代々[[興福寺]]から補任されており、神主が岡村庄の[[検断権]]を持っていたという記述がある。 明治に入ってから現在の「辛国神社」と改称され、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に[[村社]]に列せられる。[[1907年]]（明治40年）に野中村にあった村社野中神社、翌年には[[葛井寺]]内にあった藤井寺村字長野の村社長野神社を合祀する。これによって長野神社の祭神であった素盞鳴命も合祀された。その後、野中神社は[[1950年]]（昭和25年）頃に復社されている。 ==境内== 境内から入って深い緑に覆われた長い参道は1989年に「[[大阪みどりの百選]]」に選ばれている。また、参道には合祀された「長野神社」と彫られた鳥居があり、これは長野神社より移設されたものである「藤井寺市史」第一巻通史編一635頁（1997年）。 === 境内社 === *春日稲荷神社（祭神：[[宇迦之御魂大神]]） *春日天満宮（祭神：[[菅原道真]]公） == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺1-19-14 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄南大阪線]][[藤井寺駅]]下車後、徒歩約8分（南東へ約600m） == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== *[[高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|高屋神社]] - 羽曳野市にある式内社。辛国神社と同じく饒速日命を祀っており、物部氏の一支族である高屋連の本貫地であった。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/fujiidera-city/m27_02_fujiidera_karakuni.html 辛國神社 - 大阪府神社庁] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ 辛國神社 公式ホームページ] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からくにしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:藤井寺市の建築物]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]]

revision.comment: 「野中宮山古墳の後円部頂上に」等の文章の追加と校正。
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=辛国神社 |画像=[[File:Karakuni shurine 20180131.jpg|250px]]
社殿正面 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺1-19-14 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 9 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 35 | 経度秒 = 37 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神=[[饒速日命]]
[[天児屋根命]]
[[素盞鳴命]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] |創建=不詳（[[雄略天皇]]の時代か） |本殿=三間社[[流造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= 夏祭り（[[7月17日]]）
秋祭り（[[10月17日]]） |神事= }} '''辛国神社'''（からくにじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== ; 主殿 * [[饒速日命]]（ニギハヤヒノミコト） * [[天児屋根命]]（アマノコヤネノミコト） * [[素盞鳴命]]（スサノオノミコト） ; 相殿 * [[品陀別命]]（ホンダワケノミコト） * [[市杵島姫命]]（イチキシメヒメノミコト） ==歴史== 「幸国」という名称は、かつてこの地を収めていた[[物部氏]]の一支族であり、[[役小角]]の弟子であり呪術者の[[韓国広足]]を輩出した幸国連に由来している。創建は不詳であるが、[[日本書紀]]の[[雄略天皇]]13年3月条に「餌香長野邑を物部大連に賜う」とあることから、その頃に領地を賜った物部氏が祖先である[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]を祀ったことから始まり、後に幸国連が祭祀を司ったものと考えられる「藤井寺市史」第一巻通史編一638頁（1997年）。[[日本三代実録]]には[[貞観]]9年（[[867年]]）9月26日に官社に列せられたという記述があり、[[延喜式神名帳]]では小社に列している。 中世に入り、[[河内国|河内]][[守護]]である[[畠山基国]]が社領200石を寄進し、現在の地へ社殿を造営すると同時に[[奈良]]の[[春日大社]]から[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]を合祀したことから、近世にかけて「春日社」と称すようになった{{refnest|group=注釈|神社近くの「春日丘」という地名は、そこから由来するものである[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html 辛國神社（からくにじんじゃ） - 藤井寺市観光協会]2018年1月30日　閲覧。}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ 南河内屈指の長さと美しさを持つ参道　辛国神社のご紹介　〜藤井寺市〜]2018年1月30日　閲覧。[[大乗院寺社雑事記]]の[[1458年]]（長禄2年）7月1日条には神主職は岡村入道及びその先祖が代々[[興福寺]]から補任されており、神主が岡村庄の[[検断権]]を持っていたという記述がある。 [[明治]]に入ると現在の「辛国神社」に改称され、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に[[村社]]に列せられる。[[1907年]]（明治40年）に野中村にあった村社野中神社、翌年には[[葛井寺]]内にあった藤井寺村字長野の村社長野神社を合祀する。これによって長野神社の祭神であった[[スサノオ|素盞鳴命]]も合祀された。その後、野中神社は[[1950年]]（[[昭和]]25年）に[[野中宮山古墳]]の後円部頂上に復社されている。 ==境内== 境内から入って深い緑に覆われた長い参道は[[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）に「[[大阪みどりの百選]]」に選ばれている。また、参道には合祀された「長野神社」と彫られた鳥居があり、これは長野神社より移設されたものである「藤井寺市史」第一巻通史編一635頁（1997年）。 === 境内社 === *春日稲荷神社（祭神：[[宇迦之御魂大神]]） *春日天満宮（祭神：[[菅原道真]]公） File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|春日稲荷神社 File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|春日天満宮 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺1-19-14 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄南大阪線]][[藤井寺駅]]下車後、徒歩約8分（南東へ約600m） == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== *[[高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|高屋神社]] - 羽曳野市にある式内社。辛国神社と同じく饒速日命を祀っており、物部氏の一支族である高屋連の本貫地であった。 ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/fujiidera-city/m27_02_fujiidera_karakuni.html 辛國神社 - 大阪府神社庁] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ 辛國神社 公式ホームページ] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からくにしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:藤井寺市の建築物]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]]

藤井寺市観光協会]2018年1月30日　閲覧。}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ 南河内屈指の長さと美しさを持つ参道　辛国神社のご紹介　〜藤井寺市〜]2018年1月30日　閲覧。[[大乗院寺社雑事記]]の[[1458年]]（長禄2年）7月1日条には神主職は岡村入道及びその先祖が代々[[興福寺]]から補任されており、神主が岡村庄の[[検断権]]を持っていたという記述がある。 [[明治]]に入ると現在の「辛国神社」に改称され、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に[[村社]]に列せられる。[[1907年]]（明治40年）に野中村にあった村社野中神社、翌年には[[葛井寺]]内にあった藤井寺村字長野の村社長野神社を合祀する。これによって長野神社の祭神であった[[スサノオ|素盞鳴命]]も合祀された。その後、野中神社は[[1950年]]（[[昭和]]25年）に[[野中宮山古墳]]の後円部頂上に復社されている。 ==境内== 境内から入って深い緑に覆われた長い参道は[[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）に「[[大阪みどりの百選]]」に選ばれている。また、参道には合祀された「長野神社」と彫られた鳥居があり、これは長野神社より移設されたものである「藤井寺市史」第一巻通史編一635頁（1997年）。 === 境内社 === *春日稲荷神社（祭神：[[宇迦之御魂大神]]） *春日天満宮（祭神：[[菅原道真]]公） File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|春日稲荷神社 File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|春日天満宮 == 祭事 == *歳旦祭（1月1日） *節分祭（節分の日） *春日稲荷祭（春分の日） *水無月の大祓式（6月30日） *夏祭（7月17日） *御例祭（10月17日） *月次祭（毎月1日） == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺1-19-14 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄南大阪線]][[藤井寺駅]]下車後、徒歩約8分（南東へ約600m） == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== *[[高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|高屋神社]] - 羽曳野市にある式内社。辛国神社と同じく饒速日命を祀っており、物部氏の一支族である高屋連の本貫地であった。 ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/fujiidera-city/m27_02_fujiidera_karakuni.html 辛國神社 - 大阪府神社庁] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ 辛國神社 公式ホームページ] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からくにしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:藤井寺市の建築物]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]]

revision.comment: 式内社は延喜式神名帳に記された神社を指すものであり、神名帳内での神社の区分としては官幣小社が正しい（国幣・官幣のうち官幣小社はすべて畿内にあるため）。

藤井寺市観光協会]2018年1月30日　閲覧。}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ 南河内屈指の長さと美しさを持つ参道　辛国神社のご紹介　〜藤井寺市〜]2018年1月30日　閲覧。[[大乗院寺社雑事記]]の[[長禄]]2年（[[1458年]]）7月1日条には神主職は岡村入道及びその先祖が代々[[興福寺]]から補任されており、神主が岡村庄の[[検断権]]を持っていたという記述がある。 [[明治]]に入ると現在の「辛国神社」に改称され、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に[[村社]]に列せられる。[[1907年]]（明治40年）に野中村にあった村社野中神社、翌年には[[葛井寺]]内にあった藤井寺村字長野の村社長野神社を合祀する。これによって長野神社の祭神であった[[スサノオ|素盞鳴命]]も合祀された。その後、野中神社は[[1950年]]（[[昭和]]25年）に[[野中宮山古墳]]の後円部頂上に復社されている。 ==境内== 境内から入って深い緑に覆われた長い参道は[[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）に「[[大阪みどりの百選]]」に選ばれている。また、参道には合祀された「長野神社」と彫られた鳥居があり、これは長野神社より移設されたものである「藤井寺市史」第一巻通史編一635頁（1997年）。 *本殿・拝殿 *春日[[天満宮]] - 祭神：[[菅原道真]] *春日[[稲荷神社]] - 祭神：[[宇迦之御魂大神]] *社務所 File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|春日稲荷神社 File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|春日天満宮 == 祭事 == *歳旦祭（1月1日） *節分祭（節分の日） *春日稲荷祭（春分の日） *水無月の大祓式（6月30日） *夏祭（7月17日） *御例祭（10月17日） *月次祭（毎月1日） == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺1-19-14 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄南大阪線]][[藤井寺駅]]下車後、徒歩約8分（南東へ約600m） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} ==関連項目== *[[高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|高屋神社]] - [[羽曳野市]]にある式内社。辛国神社と同じく饒速日命を祀っており、物部氏の一支族である高屋連の本貫地であった。 ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai11sibu/fujiidera-city/m27_02_fujiidera_karakuni.html 辛國神社 - 大阪府神社庁] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ 辛國神社 公式ホームページ] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からくにしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:藤井寺市の建築物]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]]

御例祭（10月17日） |神事 = }} '''辛国神社'''（からくにじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == ; 主殿 * [[饒速日命]]（ニギハヤヒノミコト） * [[天児屋根命]]（アメノコヤネノミコト） * [[素盞鳴命]]（スサノオノミコト） ; 相殿 * [[市杵島姫命]]（イチキシマヒメノミコト） * [[品陀別命]]（ホンダワケノミコト） == 歴史 == 「辛国」という名称は、かつてこの地を治めていた[[物部氏]]の一支族であり、[[役小角]]の弟子であり呪術者の[[韓国広足]]を輩出した辛国連に由来している[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ 南河内屈指の長さと美しさを持つ参道　辛国神社のご紹介　〜藤井寺市〜]2022年5月21日　閲覧。創建は不詳であるが、[[日本書紀]]の[[雄略天皇]]13年3月条に「餌香長野邑を物部大連に賜う」とあることから、その頃に領地を賜った物部氏が祖先である[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]を祀ったことから始まり、辛国連が祭祀を司ったものと考えられる「藤井寺市史」第一巻通史編一638頁（1997年）。[[日本三代実録]]には[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]9年（[[867年]]）9月26日に官社に列せられたという記述があり、[[延喜式神名帳]]では官幣小社に列している。この時代には恵美坂の西南にあったようである。 中世に入り、[[河内国|河内]][[守護]]である[[畠山基国]]が社領200石を寄進し、現在の地へ社殿を造営すると同時に[[奈良]]の[[春日大社]]に祀られている[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]を合祀したことから、近世にかけて「春日社」と称すようになった{{refnest|group=注釈|神社近くの「春日丘」という地名は、そこから由来するものである[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html 辛國神社（からくにじんじゃ） - 藤井寺市観光協会]2018年1月30日　閲覧。}}。[[大乗院寺社雑事記]]の[[長禄]]2年（[[1458年]]）7月1日条には神主職は岡村入道及びその先祖が代々[[興福寺]]から補任されており、神主が岡村庄の[[検断権]]を持っていたという記述がある。 [[明治]]に入ると現在の「辛国神社」に改称され、[[1872年]]（明治5年）に[[村社]]に列せられる。[[1907年]]（明治40年）に野中村にあった村社野中神社、翌年には[[葛井寺]]内にあった藤井寺村字長野の村社長野神社を合祀する。これによって長野神社の祭神であった[[スサノオ|素盞鳴命]]も合祀された。その後、野中神社は[[1950年]]（[[昭和]]25年）に[[野中宮山古墳]]の後円部頂上に復社されている。 == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|春日稲荷神社]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|春日天満宮]] 境内から入って深い緑に覆われた長い参道は[[1989年]]（[[平成]]元年）に「[[大阪みどりの百選]]」に選ばれている。また、参道には合祀された「長野神社」と彫られた鳥居があり、これは長野神社より移設されたものである「藤井寺市史」第一巻通史編一635頁（1997年）。 * 本殿 * 拝殿 * 春日[[天満宮]] - 祭神：[[菅原道真]] * 春日[[稲荷神社]] - 祭神：[[宇迦之御魂大神]] * 社務所 * [[伊勢神宮]]遥拝所 == 祭事 == * 歳旦祭（1月1日） * 節分祭（節分の日） * 春日稲荷祭（春分の日） * 水無月の大祓式（6月30日） * 夏祭（7月17日） * 御例祭（10月17日） * 月次祭（毎月1日） == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]藤井寺1-19-14 ;交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[近鉄南大阪線]][[藤井寺駅]]下車後、徒歩約8分（南東へ約600m） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|高屋神社]] - [[羽曳野市]]にある式内社。辛国神社と同じく饒速日命を祀っており、物部氏の一支族である高屋連の本貫地であった。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html 辛國神社 - 大阪府神社庁] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ 辛國神社 公式ホームページ] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からくにしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:藤井寺市の歴史]] [[Category:藤井寺市の建築物]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]]

Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 34 |緯度秒 = 9 | N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 35 |経度秒 = 37 | E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = {{Location map|Osaka, Japan|label=Karakuni Shrine|float=none}} |祭神 = {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=饒速日命}}
{{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素盞鳴命}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small)
Formerly a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}} |創建 = During the era of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}} |本殿 = Three-bay shrine, {{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}})
Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. == 祭神 == ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} (Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == 歴史 == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867年}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907年}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}}] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[category:new pages]]

It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907年}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. == 祭神 == ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} (Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == 歴史 == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867年}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907年}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}}] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[category:new pages]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. == 祭神 == ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} (Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == 歴史 == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867年}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907年}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}}] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[category:new pages]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. == 祭神 == ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} (Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == 歴史 == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867年}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907年}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}}] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[category:new pages]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]]

As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}}] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[category:new pages]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]]

[[ja:%E8%BE%9B%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Karikuni Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Karikuni Shrine]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Karikuni Shrine]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Karikuni Shrine]] [[en:Karikuni Shrine]] [[en:Karikuni Shrine]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[ja:Karikuni Shrine]] [[en:Karikuni Shrine]] [[Category:shortest 250 pages as of Feb 24]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Shrine |名称 = {{ruby|Karakuni Shrine|からくにじんじゃ}} |画像 = {{ill|File:Karakuni shurine 20180131.jpg|ja|辛国神社 社殿|lt=Karakuni Shrine|lt_ja=辛国神社}}
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 34 |緯度秒 = 9 | N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 35 |経度秒 = 37 | E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = {{Location map|Osaka, Japan|label=Karakuni Shrine|float=none}} |祭神 = {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=饒速日命}}
{{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素盞鳴命}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small)
Formerly a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}} |創建 = During the era of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}} |本殿 = Three-bay shrine, {{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}})
Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. == 祭神 == ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} (Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == 歴史 == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867年}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907年}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}}] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[category:new pages]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]]

As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}}] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[category:new pages]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]]

[[ja:%E8%BE%9B%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Karikuni Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Karikuni Shrine]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Karikuni Shrine]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Karikuni Shrine]] [[en:Karikuni Shrine]] [[en:Karikuni Shrine]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[ja:Karikuni Shrine]] [[en:Karikuni Shrine]] [[Category:shortest 250 pages as of Feb 24]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Shrine |名称 = {{ruby|Karakuni Shrine|からくにじんじゃ}} |画像 = {{ill|File:Karakuni shurine 20180131.jpg|ja|辛国神社 社殿|lt=Karakuni Shrine|lt_ja=辛国神社}}
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 34 |緯度秒 = 9 | N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 35 |経度秒 = 37 | E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = {{Location map|Osaka, Japan|label=Karakuni Shrine|float=none}} |祭神 = {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=饒速日命}}
{{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素盞鳴命}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small)
Formerly a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}} |創建 = During the era of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}} |本殿 = Three-bay shrine, {{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}})
Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} (Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == 歴史 == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867年}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907年}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}}] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[category:new pages]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]]

As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}}] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[category:new pages]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]]

[[ja:%E8%BE%9B%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Karikuni Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Karikuni Shrine]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Karikuni Shrine]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Karikuni Shrine]] [[en:Karikuni Shrine]] [[en:Karikuni Shrine]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[ja:Karikuni Shrine]] [[en:Karikuni Shrine]] [[Category:shortest 250 pages as of Feb 24]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{Shrine |名称 = {{ruby|Karakuni Shrine|からくにじんじゃ}} |画像 = {{ill|File:Karakuni shurine 20180131.jpg|ja|辛国神社 社殿|lt=Karakuni Shrine|lt_ja=辛国神社}}
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 34 |緯度秒 = 9 | N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 35 |経度秒 = 37 | E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = {{Location map|Osaka, Japan|label=Karakuni Shrine|float=none}} |祭神 = {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=饒速日命}}
{{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素盞鳴命}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small)
Formerly a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}} |創建 = During the era of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}} |本殿 = Three-bay shrine, {{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}})
Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} (Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == 歴史 == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867年}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907年}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}}] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{DEFAULTSORT:からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[category:new pages]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Karikuni Shrine]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]]
