It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907年}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺|1=Fujii-dera}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}}] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{citation needed|からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]]

[[ja:%E8%BE%9B%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Karakuni Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[sn:arikuni Shrine]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{Shrine |名称 = {{ruby|Karakuni Shrine|からくにじんじゃ}} |image = [[File:Karakuni shurine 20180131.jpg|thumb]]|ja|辛国神社 社殿|lt=Karakuni Shrine|lt_ja=辛国神社
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 34 |緯度秒 = 9 | N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 35 |経度秒 = 37 | E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = {{Location map|Osaka, Japan|label=Karakuni Shrine|float=none}} |祭神 = {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=饒速日命}}
{{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素盞鳴命}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small)
Formerly a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}} |創建 = During the era of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}} |本殿 = Three-bay shrine, {{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}})
Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} (Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867年}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907年}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺|1=Fujii-dera}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}}] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{citation needed|からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:New]]

As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{citation needed|からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:New]]

[[ja:%E8%BE%9B%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Karakuni Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[sn:arikuni Shrine]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{Shrine |名称 = {{ruby|Karakuni Shrine|からくにじんじゃ}} |image = [[File:Karakuni shurine 20180131.jpg|thumb]]|ja|辛国神社 社殿|lt=Karakuni Shrine|lt_ja=辛国神社
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 34 |緯度秒 = 9 | N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 35 |経度秒 = 37 | E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = {{Location map|Osaka, Japan|label=Karakuni Shrine|float=none}} |祭神 = {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=饒速日命}}

Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} (Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867年}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907年}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺|1=Fujii-dera}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{citation needed|からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:New]] {{defaultsort:Karakuni Shrine}}

As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{citation needed|からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:New]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karakuni Shrine}}

Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} (Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867年}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907年}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺|1=Fujii-dera}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{citation needed|からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:New]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karakuni Shrine}}

As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{citation needed|からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:New]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karakuni Shrine}}

revision.text:
[[ja:%E8%BE%9B%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Karakuni Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[sn:arikuni Shrine]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{Shrine |名称 = {{ruby|Karakuni Shrine|からくにじんじゃ}} |image = [[File:Karakuni shurine 20180131.jpg|thumb]]|ja|辛国神社 社殿|lt=Karakuni Shrine|lt_ja=辛国神社
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 34 |緯度秒 = 9 | N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 35 |経度秒 = 37 | E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = {{Location map|Osaka, Japan|label=Karakuni Shrine|float=none}} |祭神 = {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=饒速日命}}
{{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素盞鳴命}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small)
Formerly a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}} |創建 = During the era of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}} |本殿 = Three-bay shrine, {{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}})
Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} (Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867年}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907年}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺|1=Fujii-dera}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == 境内 == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{citation needed|からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:New]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karakuni Shrine}}

As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == 祭事 == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{citation needed|からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:New]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karakuni Shrine}}

[[ja:%E8%BE%9B%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Karakuni Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[sn:arikuni Shrine]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{Shrine |名称 = {{ruby|Karakuni Shrine|からくにじんじゃ}} |image = [[File:Karakuni shurine 20180131.jpg|thumb]]|ja|辛国神社 社殿|lt=Karakuni Shrine|lt_ja=辛国神社
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 34 |緯度秒 = 9 | N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 35 |経度秒 = 37 | E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = {{Location map|Osaka, Japan|label=Karakuni Shrine|float=none}} |祭神 = {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=饒速日命}}
{{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素盞鳴命}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small)
Formerly a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}} |創建 = During the era of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}} |本殿 = Three-bay shrine, {{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}})
Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} (Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867年}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872年}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907年}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺|1=Fujii-dera}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{citation needed|からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:New]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karakuni Shrine}}

As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{citation needed|からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:New]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karakuni Shrine}}

[[ja:%E8%BE%9B%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Karakuni Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[sn:arikuni Shrine]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{Shrine |名称 = {{ruby|Karakuni Shrine|からくにじんじゃ}} |image = [[File:Karakuni shurine 20180131.jpg|thumb]]|ja|辛国神社 社殿|lt=Karakuni Shrine|lt_ja=辛国神社

Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, and its former rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} (Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867年}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872nd year }} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907年}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺|1=Fujii-dera}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{citation needed|からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:New]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karakuni Shrine}}

As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]][[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{citation needed|からくにしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:arikuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:New]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karakuni Shrine}}

[[ja:%E8%BE%9B%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Karakuni Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:Shortest 250 pages as of 2025-02-19]] [[sn:arikuni Shrine]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Karakuni Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{Shrine |名称 = {{ruby|Karakuni Shrine|からくにじんじゃ}} |image = [[File:Karakuni shurine 20180131.jpg|thumb]]|ja|辛国神社 社殿|lt=Karakuni Shrine|lt_ja=辛国神社
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 34 |緯度秒 = 9 | N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 35 |経度秒 = 37 | E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = {{Location map|Osaka, Japan|label=Karakuni Shrine|float=none}} |祭神 = {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=饒速日命}}
