Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日|12=draft|12=draft}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft|12=draft}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|12=draft|12=draft}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=draft|12=draft}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867th year|12=draft|12=draft}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺|1=Fujii-dera|12=draft|12=draft}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=draft|12=draft}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選|12=draft|12=draft}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft|12=draft}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅|12=draft|12=draft}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|12=draft|12=draft}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社|12=draft|12=draft}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{citation needed|からくにしんしや}} {{draft categories| }} Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日|12=draft|12=draft}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft|12=draft}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|12=draft|12=draft}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=draft|12=draft}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867th year|12=draft|12=draft}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺|1=Fujii-dera|12=draft|12=draft}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=draft|12=draft}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選|12=draft|12=draft}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} (Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867th year}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872nd year}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907th year}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺|1=Fujii-dera}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950th year}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989th year}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{citation needed|からくにしんしや}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E8%BE%9B%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[sn:arikuni Shrine]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]] [[ja:辛国神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karakuni Shrine}}

It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} (Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867th year}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872nd year}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907th year}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺|1=Fujii-dera}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950th year}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989th year}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮}} == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{citation needed|からくにしんしや}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Karakuni Shrine]] [[Category:pages to be reviewed for curly bracket replacement error]] [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E8%BE%9B%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[sn:arikuni Shrine]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]] [[ja:辛国神社]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Karakuni Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|辛国神社|version=90124934|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=draft|12=draft}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=draft|12=draft}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=draft|12=draft}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|12=draft|12=draft}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|12=draft|12=draft}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角|12=draft|12=draft}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=draft|12=draft}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867th year|12=draft|12=draft}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|12=draft|12=draft}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺|1=Fujii-dera|12=draft|12=draft}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=draft|12=draft}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選|12=draft|12=draft}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮|12=draft|12=draft}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=draft|12=draft}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮|12=draft|12=draft}} == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日|12=draft|12=draft}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|12=draft|12=draft}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|12=draft|12=draft}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅|12=draft|12=draft}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|12=draft|12=draft}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|12=draft|12=draft}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=draft|12=draft}}. == External Links Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{citation needed|からくにしんしや}} {{translated Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺|1=Fujii-dera|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|qq=draft|qq=draft}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺|1=Fujii-dera|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{translated It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872 AD|qq=}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Formerly a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}} |創建 = During the era of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuryaku Tennō|qq=}} |本殿 = Three-bay shrine, {{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryū-zukuri|qq=}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 17|qq=}})
Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 17|qq=}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera City|qq=}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|qq=}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaku Kogumi|qq=}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuryaku Tennō|qq=}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/867 AD|qq=}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872 AD|qq=}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907 AD|qq=}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺|1=Fujii-dera|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsui-dera|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujii-dera|qq=}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1950 AD|qq=}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989 AD|qq=}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|qq=}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmangu|qq=}} – enshrines Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Jingū|qq=}} == Festivals == * New Year's Festival Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 30|qq=}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 17|qq=}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 17|qq=}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 17|qq=}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera City|qq=}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|qq=}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaku Kogumi|qq=}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuryaku Tennō|qq=}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/867 AD|qq=}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872 AD|qq=}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907 AD|qq=}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺|1=Fujii-dera|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsui-dera|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujii-dera|qq=}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1950 AD|qq=}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989 AD|qq=}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|qq=}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmangu|qq=}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Jingū|qq=}} == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 1|qq=}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 30|qq=}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 17|qq=}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 17|qq=}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera City|qq=}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|ja|近鉄南大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line|qq=}}{{ill|Fujidera Station|ja|藤井寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Station|qq=}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzan-kaku|qq=}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|qq=}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Shrine|qq=}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{translated page|ja|辛国神社|version=90124934|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] }} [[ja:%E8%BE%9B%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[sn:arikuni Shrine]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]] [[ja:辛国神社]]

{{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Formerly a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}} |創建 = During the era of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuryaku Tennō|qq=}} |本殿 = Three-bay shrine, {{ill|Ryū-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryū-zukuri|qq=}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 17|qq=}})
Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 17|qq=}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera City|qq=}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|qq=}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaku Kogumi|qq=}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuryaku Tennō|qq=}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/867 AD|qq=}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872 AD|qq=}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907 AD|qq=}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺|1=Fujii-dera|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsui-dera|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujii-dera|qq=}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1950 AD|qq=}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989 AD|qq=}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|qq=}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmangu|qq=}} – enshrines Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|lt=Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Jingū|qq=}} Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 17|qq=}}) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera City|qq=}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|ja|物部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe clan|qq=}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|qq=}}—a disciple of {{ill|Yaku Kogumi|ja|役小角|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yaku Kogumi|qq=}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuryaku Tennō|qq=}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/867 AD|qq=}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872 AD|qq=}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907 AD|qq=}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺|1=Fujii-dera|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsui-dera|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujii-dera|qq=}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1950 AD|qq=}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989 AD|qq=}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|qq=}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmangu|qq=}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|lt=Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Jingū|qq=}}
