Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Yuryaku Tennō|ja|雄略天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuryaku Tennō|qq=}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Jōgan|ja|貞観|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgan|qq=}} ({{ill|867 AD|ja|867年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/867 AD|qq=}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji Period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Period|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872 AD|ja|1872年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872 AD|qq=}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. In {{ill|1907 AD|ja|1907年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907 AD|qq=}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Katsui-dera|ja|葛井寺|1=Fujii-dera|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsui-dera|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujii-dera|qq=}} was merged. Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950 AD|ja|1950年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1950 AD|qq=}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989 AD|ja|1989年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1989 AD|qq=}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|qq=}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangu|ja|天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmangu|qq=}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|lt=Ise Jingū|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Jingū|qq=}} == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/January 1|qq=}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|ja|6月30日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 30|qq=}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|ja|7月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 17|qq=}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|ja|10月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 17|qq=}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Fujidera City|ja|藤井寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera City|qq=}} Fujidera 1-chōme, Station|ja|藤井寺駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujidera Station|qq=}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == revision.text:
{{Shrine |名称 = {{ruby|Karakuni Shrine|からくにじんじゃ}} |image = [[File:Karakuni shurine 20180131.jpg|thumb]]|ja|辛国神社 社殿|lt=Karakuni Shrine|lt_ja=辛国神社
Haiden |所在地 = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera City]] Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 34 |緯度秒 = 9 | N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 35 |経度秒 = 37 | E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = {{Location map|Osaka, Japan|label=Karakuni Shrine|float=none}} |祭神 = {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Formerly a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}} |創建 = During the era of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yuryaku Tennō]] |本殿 = Three-bay shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryū-zukuri]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Summer Festival ([[:en:July 17|July 17]])
Special Festival ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|qq=}}—a disciple of [[:en:En no Gyōja|Yaku Kogumi]]. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yuryaku Tennō]], it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], on September 26 of the 9th year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] ([[:en:867|867 AD]]), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in [[:en:1872|1872 AD]] (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. In [[:en:1907|1907 AD]] (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in [[:en:Fujii-dera|Fujii-dera]] was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in [[:en:1950|1950 AD]] (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in [[:en:1989|1989 AD]] (the first year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|qq=}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangu]] – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Kasuga [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ([[:en:January 1|January 1]]) * Setsubun Festival (on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]]) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ([[:en:June 30|June 30]]) * Summer Festival ([[:en:July 17|July 17]]) * Special Festival ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera City]] Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]][[:en:Fujiidera Station|Fujidera Station]]; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzan-kaku|qq=}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|qq=}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Shrine|qq=}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 Special Festival ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|qq=}}—a disciple of [[:en:En no Gyōja|Yaku Kogumi]]. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yuryaku Tennō]], it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], on September 26 of the 9th year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] ([[:en:867|867 AD]]), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in [[:en:1872|1872 AD]] (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. In [[:en:1907|1907 AD]] (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in [[:en:Fujii-dera|Fujii-dera]] was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in [[:en:1950|1950 AD]] (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in [[:en:1989|1989 AD]] (the first year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|qq=}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangu]] – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Kasuga [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ([[:en:January 1|January 1]]) * Setsubun Festival (on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]]) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ([[:en:June 30|June 30]]) * Summer Festival ([[:en:July 17|July 17]]) * Special Festival ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera City]] Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]][[:en:Fujiidera Station|Fujidera Station]]; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzan-kaku|qq=}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|qq=}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. == External Links == {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Formerly a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}} |創建 = During the era of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yuryaku Tennō]] |本殿 = Three-bay shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryū-zukuri]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Summer Festival ([[:en:July 17|July 17]])
Special Festival ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|qq=}}—a disciple of [[:en:En no Gyōja|Yaku Kogumi]]. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yuryaku Tennō]], it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], on September 26 of the 9th year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] ([[:en:867|867 AD]]), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in [[:en:1872|1872 AD]] (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. In [[:en:1907|1907 AD]] (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in [[:en:Fujii-dera|Fujii-dera]] was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in [[:en:1950|1950 AD]] (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in [[:en:1989|1989 AD]] (the first year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|qq=}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangu]] – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Kasuga [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ([[:en:January 1|January 1]]) * Setsubun Festival (on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]]) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ([[:en:June 30|June 30]]) * Summer Festival ([[:en:July 17|July 17]]) * Special Festival ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera City]] Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]][[:en:Fujiidera Station|Fujidera Station]]; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzan-kaku|qq=}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|qq=}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Shrine|qq=}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{translated page|ja|辛国神社|version=90124934|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] }} [[ja:%E8%BE%9B%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[sn:arikuni Shrine]] [[sn:Karakuni Shrine]] [[ja:辛国神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine |名称 = {{ruby|Karakuni Shrine|からくにじんじゃ}} |image = [[File:Karakuni shurine 20180131.jpg|thumb]]|ja|辛国神社 社殿|lt=Karakuni Shrine|lt_ja=辛国神社
Haiden |所在地 = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera City]] Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 34 |緯度秒 = 9 | N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 35 |経度秒 = 37 | E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = {{Location map|Osaka, Japan|label=Karakuni Shrine|float=none}} |祭神 = {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Formerly a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}} |創建 = During the era of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yuryaku Tennō]] |本殿 = Three-bay shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryū-zukuri]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Summer Festival ([[:en:July 17|July 17]])
Special Festival ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|qq=}}—a disciple of [[:en:En no Gyōja|Yaku Kogumi]]. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yuryaku Tennō]], it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], on September 26 of the 9th year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] ([[:en:867|867 AD]]), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in [[:en:1872|1872 AD]] (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. In [[:en:1907|1907 AD]] (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in [[:en:Fujii-dera|Fujii-dera]] was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in [[:en:1950|1950 AD]] (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in [[:en:1989|1989 AD]] (the first year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|qq=}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangu]] – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Kasuga [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ([[:en:January 1|January 1]]) * Setsubun Festival (on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]]) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ([[:en:June 30|June 30]]) * Summer Festival ([[:en:July 17|July 17]]) * Special Festival ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera City]] Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]][[:en:Fujiidera Station|Fujidera Station]]; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzan-kaku|qq=}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|qq=}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Shrine|qq=}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{translated page|ja|辛国神社|version=90124934|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] of Fujidera City]] [[Category:Architectural Structures in Fujidera City]] [[Category:Kawachi Province's Shikinaisha]] }} [[ja:%E8%BE%9B%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Karakuni Shrine]] [[sn:arikuni Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Kasuga [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ([[:en:January 1|January 1]]) * Setsubun Festival (on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]]) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ([[:en:June 30|June 30]]) * Summer Festival ([[:en:July 17|July 17]]) * Special Festival ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; {{Shrine |名称 = {{ruby|Karakuni Shrine|からくにじんじゃ}} |image = [[File:Karakuni shurine 20180131.jpg|thumb]]|ja|辛国神社 社殿|lt=Karakuni Shrine|lt_ja=辛国神社
Haiden |所在地 = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera City]] Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 34 |緯度秒 = 9 | N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 35 |経度秒 = 37 | E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = {{Location map|Osaka, Japan|label=Karakuni Shrine|float=none}} |祭神 = {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small)
Formerly a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}} |創建 = During the era of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yuryaku Tennō]] |本殿 = Three-bay shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryū-zukuri]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Summer Festival ([[:en:July 17|July 17]])
Special Festival ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|qq=}}—a disciple of [[:en:En no Gyōja|Yaku Kogumi]]. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yuryaku Tennō]], it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], on September 26 of the 9th year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] ([[:en:867|867 AD]]), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in [[:en:1872|1872 AD]] (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. In [[:en:1907|1907 AD]] (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in [[:en:Fujii-dera|Fujii-dera]] was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in [[:en:1950|1950 AD]] (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in [[:en:1989|1989 AD]] (the first year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|qq=}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangu]] – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Kasuga [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ([[:en:January 1|January 1]]) * Setsubun Festival (on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]]) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ([[:en:June 30|June 30]]) * Summer Festival ([[:en:July 17|July 17]]) * Special Festival ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera City]] Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]][[:en:Fujiidera Station|Fujidera Station]]; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzan-kaku|qq=}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|qq=}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Shrine|qq=}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{ill|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{translated page|ja|辛国神社|version=90124934|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] Although the founding date is unknown, according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yuryaku Tennō]], it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], on September 26 of the 9th year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] ([[:en:867|867 AD]]), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in [[:en:1872|1872 AD]] (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. In [[:en:1907|1907 AD]] (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in [[:en:Fujii-dera|Fujii-dera]] was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in [[:en:1950|1950 AD]] (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in [[:en:1989|1989 AD]] (the first year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|qq=}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangu]] – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Kasuga [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ([[:en:January 1|January 1]]) * Setsubun Festival (on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]]) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ([[:en:June 30|June 30]]) * Summer Festival ([[:en:July 17|July 17]]) * Special Festival ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera City]] Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]][[:en:Fujiidera Station|Fujidera Station]]; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite Special Festival ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|qq=}}—a disciple of [[:en:En no Gyōja|Yaku Kogumi]]. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yuryaku Tennō]], it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], on September 26 of the 9th year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] ([[:en:867|867 AD]]), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in [[:en:1872|1872 AD]] (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. In [[:en:1907|1907 AD]] (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in [[:en:Fujii-dera|Fujii-dera]] was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in [[:en:1950|1950 AD]] (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in [[:en:1989|1989 AD]] (the first year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|qq=}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangu]] – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Kasuga [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ([[:en:January 1|January 1]]) * Setsubun Festival (on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]]) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ([[:en:June 30|June 30]]) * Summer Festival ([[:en:July 17|July 17]]) * Special Festival ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Line]][[:en:Fujiidera Station|Fujidera Station]]; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite Special Festival ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|qq=}}—a disciple of [[:en:En no Gyōja|Yaku Kogumi]]. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yuryaku Tennō]], it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], on September 26 of the 9th year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] ([[:en:867|867 AD]]), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in [[:en:1872|1872 AD]] (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=}}. In [[:en:1907|1907 AD]] (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in [[:en:Fujii-dera|Fujii-dera]] was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in [[:en:1950|1950 AD]] (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in [[:en:1989|1989 AD]] (the first year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|qq=}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangu]] – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Kasuga [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ([[:en:January 1|January 1]]) * Setsubun Festival (on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]]) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ([[:en:June 30|June 30]]) * Summer Festival ([[:en:July 17|July 17]]) * Special Festival ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Haiden |所在地 = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera City]] Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 34 |緯度秒 = 9 | N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 35 |経度秒 = 37 | E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = {{Location map|Osaka, Japan|label=Karakuni Shrine|float=none}} |祭神 = {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}
{{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}}
{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (small)
Formerly a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建 = During the era of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yuryaku Tennō]] |本殿 = Three-bay shrine, [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryū-zukuri]] |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Summer Festival ([[:en:July 17|July 17]])
Special Festival ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]) |神事 = }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera City]]. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the [[:en:Mononobe clan|Mononobe clan]] that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—a disciple of [[:en:En no Gyōja|Yaku Kogumi]]. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Yūryaku|Yuryaku Tennō]], it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]], on September 26 of the 9th year of [[:en:Zhenguan|Jōgan]] ([[:en:867|867 AD]]), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in [[:en:1872|1872 AD]] (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. In [[:en:1907|1907 AD]] (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in [[:en:Fujii-dera|Fujii-dera]] was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in [[:en:1950|1950 AD]] (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in [[:en:1989|1989 AD]] (the first year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|大阪みどりの百選|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|qq=}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga [[:en:Tenmangū|Tenmangu]] – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Kasuga [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrine]] – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Jingū]] == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ([[:en:January 1|January 1]]) * Setsubun Festival (on [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]]) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ([[:en:June 30|June 30]]) * Summer Festival ([[:en:July 17|July 17]]) * Special Festival ([[:en:October 17|October 17]]) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujidera City]] Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: [[:en:Minami Osaka Line|Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line]][[:en:Fujiidera Station|Fujidera Station]]; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzan-kaku|qq=}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|qq=}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Shrine|qq=}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{translated page|ja|辛国神社|version=90124934|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories|
