Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|en|October 17|WD=Q2962|lt=October 17}}) |神事 = |prefecture= {{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.569167|N|135.593611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera City}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—a disciple of {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|WD=Q198946|lt=Yaku Kogumi}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Yuryaku Tennō}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} ({{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867 AD}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872 AD}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907 AD}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Fujii-dera|en|Fujii-dera|WD=Q6406788|lt=Fujii-dera}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950|en|1950|WD=Q18597|lt=1950 AD}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989|en|1989|WD=Q2425|lt=1989 AD}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439936}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangu}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}} == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|WD=Q746798|lt=Setsubun}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|en|June 30|WD=Q2657|lt=June 30}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|en|July 17|WD=Q2729|lt=July 17}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|en|October 17|WD=Q2962|lt=October 17}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera City}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line}}{{ill|Fujiidera Station|en|Fujiidera Station|WD=Q5507418|lt=Fujidera Station}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzan-kaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|1=Takaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669282}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Shrine|qq=}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Yuryaku Tennō}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} ({{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867 AD}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872 AD}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907 AD}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Fujii-dera|en|Fujii-dera|WD=Q6406788|lt=Fujii-dera}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950|en|1950|WD=Q18597|lt=1950 AD}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989|en|1989|WD=Q2425|lt=1989 AD}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439936}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangu}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}} == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|WD=Q746798|lt=Setsubun}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|en|June 30|WD=Q2657|lt=June 30}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|en|July 17|WD=Q2729|lt=July 17}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|en|October 17|WD=Q2962|lt=October 17}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera City}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line}}{{ill|Fujiidera Station|en|Fujiidera Station|WD=Q5507418|lt=Fujidera Station}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzan-kaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|1=Takaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669282}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera City}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 34 |緯度秒 = 9 | N |経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 35 |経度秒 = 37 | E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = {{Location map|Osaka, Japan|label=Karakuni Shrine|float=none}} |祭神 = {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}

{{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Formerly a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = During the era of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Yuryaku Tennō}} |本殿 = Three-bay shrine, {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Ryū-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|en|July 17|WD=Q2729|lt=July 17}})
Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|en|October 17|WD=Q2962|lt=October 17}}) |神事 = |prefecture= {{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.569167|N|135.593611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera City}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—a disciple of {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|WD=Q198946|lt=Yaku Kogumi}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Yuryaku Tennō}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} ({{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867 AD}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872 AD}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907 AD}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Fujii-dera|en|Fujii-dera|WD=Q6406788|lt=Fujii-dera}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950|en|1950|WD=Q18597|lt=1950 AD}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989|en|1989|WD=Q2425|lt=1989 AD}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439936}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) Formerly a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = During the era of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Yuryaku Tennō}} |本殿 = Three-bay shrine, {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Ryū-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|en|July 17|WD=Q2729|lt=July 17}})
Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|en|October 17|WD=Q2962|lt=October 17}}) |神事 = |prefecture= {{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.569167|N|135.593611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera City}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—a disciple of {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|WD=Q198946|lt=Yaku Kogumi}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Yuryaku Tennō}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} ({{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867 AD}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872 AD}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907 AD}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Fujii-dera|en|Fujii-dera|WD=Q6406788|lt=Fujii-dera}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950|en|1950|WD=Q18597|lt=1950 AD}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989|en|1989|WD=Q2425|lt=1989 AD}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439936}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangu}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}} == Festivals == equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|en|June 30|WD=Q2657|lt=June 30}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|en|July 17|WD=Q2729|lt=July 17}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|en|October 17|WD=Q2962|lt=October 17}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line}}{{ill|Fujiidera Station|en|Fujiidera Station|WD=Q5507418|lt=Fujidera Station}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Formerly a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = During the era of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Yuryaku Tennō}} |本殿 = Three-bay shrine, {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Ryū-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|en|July 17|WD=Q2729|lt=July 17}})
Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|en|October 17|WD=Q2962|lt=October 17}}) |神事 = |prefecture= {{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.569167|N|135.593611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera City}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—a disciple of {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|WD=Q198946|lt=Yaku Kogumi}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Yuryaku Tennō}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} ({{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867 AD}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872 AD}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907 AD}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Fujii-dera|en|Fujii-dera|WD=Q6406788|lt=Fujii-dera}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950|en|1950|WD=Q18597|lt=1950 AD}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989|en|1989|WD=Q2425|lt=1989 AD}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439936}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangu}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}} == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|WD=Q746798|lt=Setsubun}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|en|June 30|WD=Q2657|lt=June 30}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|en|July 17|WD=Q2729|lt=July 17}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|en|October 17|WD=Q2962|lt=October 17}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera City}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line}}{{ill|Fujiidera Station|en|Fujiidera Station|WD=Q5507418|lt=Fujidera Station}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—a disciple of {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|WD=Q198946|lt=Yaku Kogumi}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Yuryaku Tennō}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} ({{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867 AD}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872 AD}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907 AD}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Fujii-dera|en|Fujii-dera|WD=Q6406788|lt=Fujii-dera}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950|en|1950|WD=Q18597|lt=1950 AD}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989|en|1989|WD=Q2425|lt=1989 AD}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439936}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Yuryaku Tennō}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} ({{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867 AD}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} |社格 = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
Formerly a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = During the era of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Yuryaku Tennō}} |本殿 = Three-bay shrine, {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Ryū-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|en|July 17|WD=Q2729|lt=July 17}})
Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|en|October 17|WD=Q2962|lt=October 17}}) |神事 = |prefecture= {{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.569167|N|135.593611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera City}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—a disciple of {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|WD=Q198946|lt=Yaku Kogumi}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Yuryaku Tennō}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} ({{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867 AD}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872 AD}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907 AD}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Fujii-dera|en|Fujii-dera|WD=Q6406788|lt=Fujii-dera}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950|en|1950|WD=Q18597|lt=1950 AD}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989|en|1989|WD=Q2425|lt=1989 AD}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439936}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangu}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}} == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|WD=Q746798|lt=Setsubun}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|en|June 30|WD=Q2657|lt=June 30}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|en|July 17|WD=Q2729|lt=July 17}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|en|October 17|WD=Q2962|lt=October 17}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera City}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line}}{{ill|Fujiidera Station|en|Fujiidera Station|WD=Q5507418|lt=Fujidera Station}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Formerly a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = During the era of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Yuryaku Tennō}} |本殿 = Three-bay shrine, {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Ryū-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|en|July 17|WD=Q2729|lt=July 17}})
Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|en|October 17|WD=Q2962|lt=October 17}}) |神事 = |prefecture= {{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.569167|N|135.593611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Karakuni Shrine''' (辛国神社, からくにじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera City}}. It is classified as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, and its former Rank was that of a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} ; Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—a disciple of {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|WD=Q198946|lt=Yaku Kogumi}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Yuryaku Tennō}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} ({{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867 AD}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872 AD}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907 AD}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Fujii-dera|en|Fujii-dera|WD=Q6406788|lt=Fujii-dera}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950|en|1950|WD=Q18597|lt=1950 AD}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989|en|1989|WD=Q2425|lt=1989 AD}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439936}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangu}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}} == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|WD=Q746798|lt=Setsubun}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|en|June 30|WD=Q2657|lt=June 30}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|en|July 17|WD=Q2729|lt=July 17}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|en|October 17|WD=Q2962|lt=October 17}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera City}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line}}{{ill|Fujiidera Station|en|Fujiidera Station|WD=Q5507418|lt=Fujidera Station}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzan-kaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}|date=August 1932}} == See wikidata|WD=Q11669282}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} Main Hall * {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}} (Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} (Ameno-koyane-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto) ; Side Hall * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} (Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto) * {{ill|Hondawake-no-Mikoto|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hondawake-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Hondawake-no-Mikoto) == History == The name "Karakuni" derives from a branch of the {{ill|Mononobe clan|en|Mononobe clan|WD=Q1755836|lt=Mononobe clan}} that once ruled this area and produced the shamanistic figure {{ill|Karakuni Renzu|ja|辛国連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Renzu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}—a disciple of {{ill|En no Gyōja|en|En no Gyōja|WD=Q198946|lt=Yaku Kogumi}}. Although the founding date is unknown, according to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}} in the entry for the 13th year, March of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Yuryaku Tennō}}, it states that "Eka Chōno-mura was granted to Mononobe Ōren," indicating that the Mononobe clan, upon receiving these lands, enshrined their ancestor {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, from which Karakuni Renzu later assumed responsibility for the rites.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}} According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, on September 26 of the 9th year of {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jōgan}} ({{ill|867|en|867|WD=Q30347|lt=867 AD}}), the shrine was listed among the government-supported shrines, and it is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a government-issued small shrine. It appears that during this period the shrine was located to the southwest of 【Emisaka】.[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, the shrine was renamed "Karakuni Shrine" and, in {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872 AD}} (Meiji 5), it was designated as a {{ill|Murasya|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murasya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907 AD}} (Meiji 40), the village shrine of Nonaka in Nonaka Village was merged, and the following year, the village shrine of Nagano in Fujidera (from the Nagano area) housed in {{ill|Fujii-dera|en|Fujii-dera|WD=Q6406788|lt=Fujii-dera}} was merged. As a result, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} of Nagano Shrine was also enshrined.{{Cite book|lang=ja|title=Fujidera City History|volume=Volume 1, General History, page 638|date=1997}}[http://travel-minakawa.com/2018/01/01/post-917/ Southern Kawachi’s most beautiful and long approach – Introduction to Karakuni Shrine ~ Fujidera City ~] (Accessed May 21, 2022) Subsequently, Nonaka Shrine was re-established atop the posterior circular part of Nonaka Miyashiro Kofun in {{ill|1950|en|1950|WD=Q18597|lt=1950 AD}} (Showa 25). == Shrine facilities == [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-inari-jinja.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Inari Shrine]] [[File:Karakuni-jinja, Kasuga-tenmangu.jpg|thumb|200px|Kasuga Tenmangu]] Within the shrine grounds, there is a long approach (参道) covered in deep green foliage, which was selected in {{ill|1989|en|1989|WD=Q2425|lt=1989 AD}} (the first year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}) as one of the {{ill|Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|ja|100 Greens of Ōsaka|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Midori no Hyakusen|1=Ōsaka Midori no Hyakusen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11439936}}.[http://www.fujiidera-kanko.info/spot/karakuni.html Karakuni Shrine – Fujidera City Tourism Association] (Accessed January 30, 2018) Additionally, along the approach there is a torii bearing the inscription "Nagano Shrine," which was relocated from Nagano Shrine.「Fujidera City History」, Volume 1, General History, page 635 (1997) * Main Hall * Haiden (worship hall) * Kasuga {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|WD=Q798838|lt=Tenmangu}} – enshrines {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Kasuga {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari Shrine}} – enshrines {{ill|Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja|宇迦之御魂大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama-no-Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ウカノミタマ|1=Ukanomitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080728}} * Administrative Office * A remote worship area for {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|WD=Q687168|lt=Ise Jingū}} == Festivals == * New Year's Festival ({{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1}}) * Setsubun Festival (on {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|WD=Q746798|lt=Setsubun}}) * Spring Inari Festival (on the vernal equinox) * Great Purification Ritual of Mizu-nazuki ({{ill|June 30|en|June 30|WD=Q2657|lt=June 30}}) * Summer Festival ({{ill|July 17|en|July 17|WD=Q2729|lt=July 17}}) * Special Festival ({{ill|October 17|en|October 17|WD=Q2962|lt=October 17}}) * Monthly Festival (on the 1st day of every month) == Local Information == ; Address: * {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujidera City}} Fujidera 1-chōme, 19-14 ; Transportation: * Nearest Station: {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|WD=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami-Osaka Line}}{{ill|Fujiidera Station|en|Fujiidera Station|WD=Q5507418|lt=Fujidera Station}}; approximately an 8‑minute walk (about 600 m to the southeast). == Footnotes == {{Footnotes Help}} {{Reflist}} == References == * {{cite book|lang=ja|title=Great Japan Geography Series|volume=Volume 38, New Edition Sagami Fudoki-kō, 3rd volume, Section 67 of Village Areas, Takaza District, Volume 9|chapter=Shibuyashō, Oshima Village, Suwa-sha|id={{NDLJP|1179219/184}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Sagami Fudoki-kō, Oshima Village Suwa-sha|ja|新編相模国風土記稿 大島村 諏訪社}}|publisher={{ill|Yuzan-kaku|ja|雄山閣|lt=Yuzan-kaku|lt_ja=雄山閣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuzan-kaku|1=Yuzankaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11659206}}|date=August 1932}} == See Also == * {{ill|Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|ja|高屋神社 (羽曳野市)|lt=Takaya Shrine|lt_ja=高屋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaya Shrine (Hayabusa City)|1=Takaya Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11669282}} – Located in Hayabusa City, it also enshrines {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Karakuni-jinja}} *[https://www.osaka-jinjacho.jp/funai_jinja/dai11shibu/fujiidera-city/11008karakunijinja.html {{ill|Karakuni Shrine|ja|辛国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karakuni Shrine|qq=}} - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency] *[http://www.karakunijinjya.jp/ {{bad link|These are causing so much issues|Karakuni Shrine Official Homepage|ja|辛国神社 公式ホームページ}]] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{osaka-green-100}} {{translated page|ja|辛国神社|version=90124934|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Karakuni-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Karakuni Shrine (Q48762570)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !!
